rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION: $1,211.00 ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order For Terminating Sanctions And Striking Claim
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 10, 2025, Line 1, PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA's Motion For Order For Terminating Sanctions And Striking Claim. 1. Motion timely filed and unopposed. 2. Motion granted as requested. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/BZ).

rulings.Case Title: JASON COOK VS. CF COLLECTIVE, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, January 10, 2025, line 4, 1 - DEFENDANTS CF COLLECTIVE, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY CORPORATION, FORMERLY DOING BUSINESS AS CROSSFIT POTRERO HILL, JOSEPH TALMADGE, ARLENE TALMADGE, JOSEPH TALMADGE, ALEXIS GARROD, HOPPER LIFE LLC, TALMADGE & TALMADGE, INC. DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* 1. Plaintiff's objection to the reply request for judicial notice/sustained 3. Demurrer to the second cause of action for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing/Overruled. 4. Demurrer to the fifth cause of action for fraudulent transfer/Overruled 5. Demurrer to the sixth cause of action for declaratory relief/Overruled. 6. Demurrer to the "unjust enrichment" cause of action/Sustained/ twenty days leave to amend. 7. Demurrers to the Labor Code claims (causes of action ten through thirteen)/ overruled. 8. Demurrer to the fourteenth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress/Sustained/ twenty days leave to amend. 9. Demurrer to the fifteenth cause of action for unfair competition/Sustained with leave to amend to clarify the unlawful business practices. 10. Demurrer to the sixteenth cause of action for buyout/Overruled. 11. Demurrer to the seventeenth cause of action/Sustained/ twenty days leave to amend. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/BZ)

rulings.Case Title: JASON COOK VS. CF COLLECTIVE, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, January 10, 2025, line 4, 2 - DEFENDANTS JOSEPH TALMADGE , INDIVIDUALS, ARLENE TALMADGE, JOSEPH TALMADGE, ALEXIS GARROD, CF COLLECTIVE, HOPPER LIFE LLC, TALMADGE & TALMADGE, INC'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND Amended COMPLAINT Motion to strike/granted and denied in part. 1. Motion/Denied as to the punitive damages allegations. a. Court liberally construes the pleading per CCP 452. b. Furthermore, Plaintiff pleads sufficient facts to support the claim. 2. Motion/Denied as to attorney's fees. a. Plaintiff's alter ego allegations are sufficient. b. Plaintiff can seek fees under section 13.19 of the operating agreement. c. Moreover, "[t]here is no requirement that the intent to seek attorney fees under section 1021.5 must be pleaded in the underlying action. [Citation.] Such fees are not part of the underlying cause of action, but are incidents to the cause and are properly awarded after entry of a ... judgment[.]" (Washburn v. City of Berkeley (1987) 195 Cal.App.3d 578, 583; see also Snatchko v. Westfield LLC (2010) 187 Cal.App.4th 469, 497 [same and noting error to grant motion to strike the request].) 2. Plaintiff's allegations regarding the home purchase are not false, irrelevant, or improper per CCP 436. They are examples of the alleged self-dealing. a. Motion/Denied as to these allegations. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/BZ) The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/BZ)

rulings.Case Title: MIDLAND CREDIT MANAGEMENT, INC. VS. JOHN HAMBLETT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, January 10, 2025, line 5, DEFENDANT JOHN HAMBLETT'S Motion To Quash Service; Request For Sanctions 1. Although the motion is unopposed, Court records reflect the defendant was served Oct. 27, 2024. POS reflects the person being served acknowledged he was the person named in the papers being served. 2. POS of service also reflects "The individual tried to refuse service by dropping documents and did not state reason for refusal. 3. Motion to quash/denied.
